heroHeadline: Day 27: CJNG 'Operates Across All of Mexico' — García Harfuch; Arms Network Sought Missiles for Cartel
heroSubtitle: Day 27 since El Mencho's killing at Tapalpa. Security Secretary Omar García Harfuch publicly acknowledged CJNG 'operates as a criminal organization across the entire country' despite losing its founder and 37+ senior figures. O3 (Juan Carlos Valencia González) remains the reported successor, but consolidation is incomplete with El Jardinero retaining regional power. Three significant developments on Day 27: (1) U.S. DOJ exposed an international weapons-trafficking ring — involving a Bulgarian intermediary — that sought to supply CJNG with missiles and military explosives, underscoring the cartel's effort to upgrade Grupo Élite's paramilitary capability during the succession crisis; (2) FGR acknowledged evidence contamination at properties where El Mencho's narconómina (payroll ledger) was discovered, raising concerns about the integrity of key financial intelligence; (3) Operativo Enjambre in Michoacán detained five Chavinda municipal officials with alleged CJNG ties, seizing a tracked vehicle, weapons, narcotics, and CJNG insignia. In Guerrero, an AP report documented self-defense groups rearming against La Nueva Familia Michoacana — with former CJNG members noted among the vigilantes — illustrating the post-Mencho territorial fragmentation now playing out across multiple states.
footerNote: Data sourced from SEDENA, SSPC, FGR, DEA, U.S. DOJ, InSight Crime, Crisis Group, Reuters, AP, Excélsior, Infobae México, La Silla Rota, Janes, and major Mexican news outlets. All contested figures noted. Last update: March 21, 2026.
